plainEnglish: At Level AAA, your content imposes no time limits at all on interactions (except non-interactive media and real-time events like a live webinar). Users can take as long as they need to read content and complete tasks.
whyItMatters: Users who need significantly more time—due to cognitive, motor, or reading disabilities—are excluded by any non-essential time limit on a task.
quickCheck: Confirm no interactive task in your app imposes a time limit other than live or real-time events.
commonMistakes: Imposing a countdown on quizzes or checkout flows that isn't strictly essential.
Auto-logging out idle users when the activity doesn't truly require it.
Auto-advancing slideshows or steps that move on before the user has finished.

codeExamples.label: No countdown — the user submits when ready
codeExamples.code:
// No timer: the form is submitted only when the user chooses
<form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>{/* ... */}</form>

codeExamples.label: Quiz that auto-submits on a timer
